reasons: Akai's Headbutt knock-up and Heavy Spin ultimate displace and disrupt enemies, and a wall-pin during Heavy Spin holds a target still so Alucard can free-hit with Whirling Smash and Pursuit dashes.
His tankiness soaks enemy CC and frontline damage, opening the dive window Alucard needs before his lifesteal engine starts.

proof.summary: Akai initiates with his Heavy Spin ultimate, shoving a key target against terrain in a wall-pin that briefly stuns on collision and holds them through the spin; Alucard dives the held target and chains skills into passive gap-close attacks without being kited, while Akai absorbs the enemy lockdown that would otherwise pin Alucard.
worksBestWhen: Akai engages first and spends enemy CC before Alucard enters.
The target gets cornered against a wall.
Alucard activates Fission Wave on entry for the lifesteal and cooldown boost.
failureCases: Enemy has multiple dashes or blinks to escape the spin.
Alucard dives before Akai locks the target and eats the CC himself.
Heavy anti-heal shuts down his sustain mid-fight.

reasons: Kaja's Divine Judgment is an uncancellable suppression that drags a backline target into the team, handing Alucard a stationary, escape-proof target to burst.
Suppression bypasses Purify and immunity, so the locked carry cannot dash away from Alucard's Pursuit.

proof.summary: Kaja flies in and suppresses a priority carry, dragging them back toward his team where they are stuck and unable to act; Alucard, who lacks reliable CC and is normally kited, gets a guaranteed stationary target to delete with Whirling Smash and Fission Wave before the suppression ends.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches an isolated squishy backliner.
Alucard is in range to follow up immediately on the dragged target.
The team converges on the suppressed target.
failureCases: Kaja gets caught and bursted before reaching the carry.
Enemy frontline peels Alucard off the suppressed target.
Diggie CC immunity is active when Kaja casts.

reasons: Grock's Wild Charge and ult disrupt enemy formation, and his Guardian's Barrier wall blocks escape routes so the target cannot flee Alucard's lock-on.
His durable frontline tanks engage and CC, creating the safe dive lane Alucard needs.

proof.summary: Grock charges in to scatter and stun the enemy, then drops Guardian's Barrier to wall off retreat paths; trapped targets cannot reposition out of Alucard's Pursuit range, letting his lifesteal-fueled melee combo run uninterrupted while Grock soaks the frontline.
worksBestWhen: Grock walls behind the target to cut escape.
Grock engages first to spend enemy CC.
Fights occur in tight terrain where the wall is most effective.
failureCases: Enemy has dashes or blinks over or around the wall.
Grock is out of position so the wall lands uselessly.
Alucard is focused by burst before sustain kicks in.

reasons: Angela's Heartguard ult shield and her Smart Heart movement-speed passive let her keep Alucard topped up and protected while he dives deep into the backline.
The shield and movement-speed boost cover Alucard's lack of an escape, extending his time inside the fight.

proof.summary: Angela attaches a shield and movement-speed boost to Alucard and can ult into him for global protection, stacking on top of his innate lifesteal so he survives the focus fire that normally punishes his no-escape dives, keeping his attack uptime high so his sustain engine keeps running.
worksBestWhen: Angela times the shield as Alucard enters the fight.
Alucard commits to a sustained brawl rather than a quick poke.
Angela stays in shield range of the diving Alucard.
failureCases: Enemy applies heavy anti-heal that negates both shield value and his lifesteal.
Alucard dives so deep Angela cannot reach him.
Burst combo kills Alucard faster than the shield absorbs.

reasons: Selena's Abyssal Arrow stuns a target from long range, setting up a clean pick that Alucard can dive in and finish.
Her Abyssal Trap vision warns Alucard of ambushes, protecting his squishy dive approach.

proof.summary: Selena lands a long-range Abyssal Arrow stun whose duration scales with distance, locking a target in place; Alucard converts that lockdown into a free dive-and-burst with his passive gap-closer, and her traps give the map vision that keeps his fragile approach safe from counter-ambush.
worksBestWhen: Selena hits a full-charge long-range arrow for max stun.
Alucard is positioned to follow up before the stun expires.
Trap vision reveals the target's location ahead of the engage.
failureCases: The stunned target is too far for Alucard to reach in time.
Enemy peels or cleanses before Alucard arrives.
